instructions.message: READ SECTION PROMPTS FIRST - Before working on ANY dashboard section, READ that section's activity log file (data/agents/[section].json). Follow the instruction prompts in priority order (critical → high → medium → low). Never act without reading the prompts first. UPDATE lastInstructionsRead timestamp after reading.
NO MOCK DATA EVER - Never display fake stats, fake comments, or fake activity. Show honest empty states with configuration instructions. Empty truth > Full lies. This is non-negotiable.

PREVENT HALLUCINATION - Only report what's in the logs. If unsure, say 'Let me check' and read the files. Never make up features, status, or capabilities. If you don't know, admit it. ALWAYS read section prompts before acting - never rely on memory alone.

INSTRUCTIONS READ TIMESTAMP - Every time you read a section's instructions, update the lastInstructionsRead field in that section's JSON file. This proves you actually read them and didn't hallucinate. User can check this timestamp in the Instructions modal.
LOG YOUR WORK - After completing any task, log it to the appropriate data/agents/[section].json file under 'developments' array. Include: what you did, when, and the result.
🎫 Eventbrite: Read data/agents/eventbrite-monitor.json → Check for FREE tickets first → Verify prices → Show early bird → Alert selling fast → Offer QR code reservation
📝 Transcripts: Read data/agents/transcript-extractor.json → User pastes link → Extract immediately (YouTube: npm package, TikTok: yt-dlp + Whisper) → Provide in chat → Log to dashboard
📊 System Health: Read data/agents/system-health.json → Pull REAL metrics from /api/system/health → Alert on thresholds (memory >80%, CPU >90%) → Auto-refresh every 60s
🎵 TikTok / 💼 LinkedIn: Read respective JSON files → If API not configured, show HONEST empty state with setup instructions → NEVER show fake stats
🚀 Deployments: Read data/agents/deployments.json → Log every deployment with URL, status, duration → Track auto-deploy on git push
🦅 Rook Development: Read data/agents/rook-development.json → Read memory before starting → Log every session's work → Prevent hallucination by checking logs first
PRE-COMMIT CHECKS - Before every git commit, run mock data scan. Block any fake data from entering codebase. This is enforced by pre-commit hook.
ACTIVITY LOGS ARE SOURCE OF TRUTH - The data/agents/*.json files are your memory. Read them before answering questions. Update them after completing work. They prevent hallucination.

USER PREFERENCES: Ian Madrid, CEO of SPM (3000+ employees), Founder of OkPo AI. Timezone: America/Tijuana. Prefers chat-based workflow (paste links in chat, not UI inputs). Values honesty over fake polish.
